audit_label: PUBLIC CLIENT-ZERO PROOF v0.1 — Case Study #2 · Governed replacement / wrap. Internal demonstration only. Not a Fortune-500 deployment claim.

case_study.title: Governed Application Replacement — Client-Zero / Internal Demonstration
case_study.label: Client-Zero / Internal Demonstration
case_study.story: Noetfield's buyer-facing Trust Ledger workspace at /workspace/ was served as a static mock that misrepresented the real governance console. This case study records the retain/wrap/replace decision, failed verification, repair, independent re-verification, promotion, and live re-proof — without claiming external enterprise production maturity.

workflow_under_change.name: Public Trust Ledger Workspace entry path
workflow_under_change.before: Static www HTML / mock workspace presentation — no Next.js app shell on the public buyer path
workflow_under_change.after: WRAP: public /workspace proxies to the live governance-console sandbox (Next.js) with independent live gates

decision.why_not_retain: RETAIN would keep a static mock on a buyer CTA path — classified as a critical product regression (incident INCIDENT-2026-07-06-001).
decision.why_not_full_replace: Full REPLACE of the static www host with the Next.js console on Cloudflare Pages was larger than needed; the real console already existed as a deployable service.
decision.why_wrap: WRAP preserves the public www path buyers already use, while serving the real governance-console UI and API through a bounded proxy to a dedicated sandbox runtime.

lifecycle.summary: Before repair, public /workspace did not serve the Next.js Trust Ledger Workspace app shell.

evidence.description: Founder-classified critical regression: static mock / missing live workspace on www
Documented curl of www /workspace returned static HTML without _next / Trust Ledger Workspace markers

lifecycle.summary: WRAP selected over RETAIN (unacceptable mock) and full REPLACE (oversized for this bounded surface).

evidence.description: Gov proxy enabled for /workspace and related API prefixes

lifecycle.summary: Acceptance criteria: public /workspace must serve Next.js _next assets; must not contain nf-workspace-mock; /tle must return 2xx; Railway health must identify governance-console-api.

evidence.description: No interactive downgrade of live client sandbox surfaces

lifecycle.summary: Bounded deploy of gov-sandbox-api + gov-sandbox-web and www proxy enablement — not a full Motor rewrite.
2026-07-06 verification failed: public buyer path lacked Next.js shell and presented mock workspace semantics.

failure.detected_by: Founder review + live curl inspection of www /workspace and /copilot/demo mock markers
failure.failure_mode: Static / mock presentation on a path labelled as workspace; Open workspace CTA did not reach real Trust Ledger Workspace

lifecycle.summary: Deployed sandbox runtime services and enabled www proxy so /workspace serves the real console shell.
lifecycle.repaired_by: Founder-directed corrective actions closed in incident resolution (2026-07-07)

lifecycle.summary: Independent gate scripts (not the builder narrative) re-ran and passed on live capture.
lifecycle.independence_note: verify-www-live-sandbox.sh and verify-gov-sandbox-railway.sh are separate fail-closed gates from the HTML/marketing surface.

verify-www-live-sandbox.sh.stdout_tail:
=== verify-www-live-sandbox ===
OK   https://www.noetfield.com/workspace/ serves Next.js app shell (_next)
OK   https://www.noetfield.com/workspace/ has: Trust Ledger Workspace
OK   https://www.noetfield.com/workspace/ has: Create TLE draft
OK   workspace is not static mock
OK   https://www.noetfield.com/tle returns 200

verify-www-live-sandbox passed.

verify-gov-sandbox-railway.sh.stdout_tail:
=== verify-gov-sandbox-railway ===
OK   https://gov-sandbox-api-production.up.railway.app/health governance-console-api
OK   https://gov-sandbox-web-production.up.railway.app/workspace has Next.js shell
OK   Railway gov-sandbox-api deployment SUCCESS
OK   Railway gov-sandbox-web deployment SUCCESS
wrote reports/agent-auto/events/nf-gov-sandbox-v1.json

verify-gov-sandbox-railway passed.

lifecycle.summary: Founder closed the incident after live gate PASS — treating the wrapped /workspace path as the promoted buyer sandbox entry.
This public bundle plus live capture receipt and gate stdout constitute the final public receipt for Client-Zero Case Study #2 v0.1.
Calendar repair window is evidenced; dollar ROI and total engineering hours are not measured and are not invented.

value_demonstrated: Buyer /workspace path serves real Next.js Trust Ledger Workspace markers (DEMONSTRATED on live capture)
Static mock marker nf-workspace-mock absent on /workspace (DEMONSTRATED)
Independent live gates exit 0 (PROVEN on capture run_id)

capabilities.name: Retain / wrap / replace decision with rationale

limitations: Internal client-zero demonstration — not an external customer engagement.
Does not claim Fortune-500 or regulated production maturity.
Cost/time dollar metrics are not measured in this version.
Does not implement the full Motor software-factory product.
Some historical failure evidence is incident-backed; contiguous machine hashes begin at the live capture receipt.
next_proof: Measure engineering hours and hosting cost for a second wrap/replace candidate.
Run one bounded internal Replace (not only Wrap) with the same fail→repair→promote receipt pattern.
Package Option 2 outreach memo from this public proof for a named enterprise/frontier operator.

attachments.label: Application Factory offer (separate narrative page — not this proof)

exclusions_note: This public bundle excludes: private repository filesystem paths, credentials, secrets, personal data, undisclosed commercial information, raw agent traces, and any fabricated metrics or timestamps.

integrity.note: sha256 of canonical JSON with integrity.digest set to empty string
